urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-33C4-00000-00HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:01ZLexisNexisSTOCKHENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO

PermID4296577863Websitehttp://www.henselphelps.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

Hensel Phelps Construction Co., Inc., founded in 1937, is an employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Greeley, Colorado. With over 4,000 employees across 10 U.S. offices, it delivers construction and renovation projects in aviation, commercial, healthcare, education, and government sectors. Generating over $6 billion annually, notable projects include the Pentagon renovation and San Jose International Airport modernization. In 2024, Hensel Phelps advanced its use of virtual design and construction (VDC) technologies, with VDC Manager Mark Blacklin winning a drone challenge for high-quality imaging. Its culture emphasizes safety, collaboration, and innovation, fostering a commitment to excellence through employee ownership. Competing with Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps stands out for its integrated project delivery and sustainable practices. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy projects and enhancing safety programs like Construction Safety Week.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ HENSEL PHELPS SIGNS MULTI-YEAR DEAL WITH TRACK3D TO STANDARDIZE CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MONITORING ACROSS PORTFOLIO

Track3D and Hensel Phelps have signed a multi-year agreement to roll out Track3D's Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, elevating a pilot into a portfolio-wide deployment as of Apr. 1, 2026. The deal underscores Hensel Phelps' commitment to leveraging AI-driven construction data across its operations.The arrangement envisions standardizing Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier identification of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex projects, while improving schedule reliability and accountability across diverse sites.A San Francisco International Airport case study showcased the platform's ability to move teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and active involvement by existing field personnel, minimizing workflow disruption.In its March update, Track3D introduced an admin dashboard, Groups functionality and capture-free progress tracking directly on floorplans, designed to give major contractors centralized visibility and easier user management.The company is expanding its market approach by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026 to demonstrate automation of production tracking and reduction of manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Taken together, the developments reinforce Track3D's competitive position and lay groundwork for future growth.

+ MICAH (PONO) CHOY NAMED PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT FOR HAWAII REGIONAL KITCHEN AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, the promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.Choy's experience spans aviation, wastewater, and federal market projects in Hawaii and Guam, where he has helped deliver complex assignments from planning to execution.Choy's collaborative leadership has allowed him to build strong partnerships across teams and consistently drive project outcomes. Hensel Phelps regards this promotion as part of its ongoing effort to develop talent from within; the Regional Kitchen project is essential for upgrading Hawaii's educational facilities and will benefit from his leadership.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ADOPTS TRACK3D REALITY INTELLIGENCE FOR PORTFOLIO-WIDE PROGRESS MONITORING

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable application, Track3D pointed to a San Francisco International Airport case study where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes.On the development front, Track3D disclosed a March release to boost enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency, including an admin dashboard and Groups functionality to centralize portfolio visibility and streamline user management, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, enhancing operational flexibility for enterprise users.The company is also sharpening its go-to-market by targeting specialty contractors and will participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in the industry as it aims to automate production tracking and protect margins. Together with the Hensel Phelps deal and the SFO case study, the March upgrades mark meaningful commercial traction for Track3D.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN CREATE MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC. IN STRATEGIC MERGER

Post-merger ownership is projected to be about 20% for Auddia and 80% for Thramann, subject to a minimum cash balance of $12 million at closing. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann carries a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million and features flexible conversion and redemption terms, affording Thramann substantial governance influence over the merged entity.

+ CALIFORNIA TOWER TOPPING OUT SIGNALS PROGRESS IN UC DAVIS HEALTH MASTER PLAN

The California Tower is a 14-story hospital with a five-story pavilion designed to house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities for pharmacy and burn care, and will add about 334 inpatient beds. The tower is part of UC Davis Health's eight-year plan to double the campus size — the largest health-system capital expansion underway in the United States — with opening anticipated in 2030 as the project advances under Vision 2030. The initiative will expand campus space from roughly 3.6 million square feet to more than 7 million.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS DETAILS 2024 EMISSIONS FOOTPRINT IN COMPREHENSIVE GHG REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction companies in the United States, released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, published on 16th February 2026. Developed in partnership with Gravity, the report offers a thorough view of the company’s carbon footprint, covering Scope 1, Scope 2 and key Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 undergoing independent auditing and limited assurance.The publication aligns with McCarthy’s mission to strengthen sustainability across its operations and subsidiaries and to uphold the standard of being the best builder in America. The company’s sustainability framework rests on three priorities: understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly for people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption throughout projects and operations.McCarthy also describes how emissions data informs broader responsible practices, including biodiversity initiatives and responsible fuel management. The organization leverages digital tools and artificial intelligence to improve project decisions and efficiency. A notable initiative is its PV recycling program for solar panels, conducted with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and advance a circular economy.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-43B8-00000-00HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:01ZLexisNexisSTOCKHENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO

PermID4296577863Websitehttp://www.henselphelps.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

Hensel Phelps Construction Co., Inc., founded in 1937, is an employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Greeley, Colorado. With over 4,000 employees across 10 U.S. offices, it delivers construction and renovation projects in aviation, commercial, healthcare, education, and government sectors. Generating over $6 billion annually, notable projects include the Pentagon renovation and San Jose International Airport modernization. In 2024, Hensel Phelps advanced its use of virtual design and construction (VDC) technologies, with VDC Manager Mark Blacklin winning a drone challenge for high-quality imaging. Its culture emphasizes safety, collaboration, and innovation, fostering a commitment to excellence through employee ownership. Competing with Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps stands out for its integrated project delivery and sustainable practices. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy projects and enhancing safety programs like Construction Safety Week.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. His ability to navigate complex projects while fostering teamwork aligns with the company's strategic goals.As Hensel Phelps embarks on new initiatives, Choy's leadership will likely contribute to the successful execution of high-profile projects. Stakeholders and investors will be watching closely as the company continues to build its reputation in the construction sector.

+ TRACK3D PARTNERS WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR MAJOR CONSTRUCTION ROLLOUT

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has announced a significant milestone in its growth trajectory as it secures a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps. As of 25th March 2026, this collaboration expands from a single pilot project to deployment across more than 200 active projects, marking a notable shift in how the construction industry embraces advanced technology.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform portfolio-wide for progress monitoring, demonstrating a strong confidence in the technology's scalability across diverse project types. This extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue streams for Track3D, enhance customer loyalty, and establish a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In addition to the enterprise agreement, Track3D highlighted a successful case study at San Francisco International Airport. Hensel Phelps utilized Track3D's technology on this demanding build, where project leaders noted that the platform facilitated a shift from reactive to proactive decision-making. The deployment at SFO showcases how Track3D can enhance progress tracking and coordination across trades without necessitating significant changes to existing workflows.On the product development front, Track3D has announced a March release aimed at improving enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. New features include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which allow for centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, enhancements such as capture-free progress tracking capabilities enable teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, marking a significant improvement in operational flexibility for enterprise users.Track3D is also advancing its go-to-market strategy by focusing on specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. This event is pivotal for drywall and ceiling professionals, and Track3D aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and enhance margin protection. Overall, the recent developments with Hensel Phelps, the SFO case study, and the March product upgrades indicate a period of meaningful commercial traction for Track3D, potentially solidifying its competitive position in the construction technology landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION MARKS KEY MILESTONE WITH ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER

FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JJX-DGD3-RSP4-R3BC-00000-00Sempra (NYSE:SRE) Sells 45% Infrastructure Stake to KKR for $10 Billion2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:01ZLexisNexisSempra (NYSE:SRE) Sells 45% Infrastructure Stake to KKR for $10 Billion

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

12 May 2026 14:15 EDT

On 10th May 2026, Sempra (NYSE:SRE) divests 45% of its infrastructure unit to private equity firm KKR for $10,000,000,000, a move designed to simplify its business and bolster its balance sheet. The infrastructure unit being sold includes LNG terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra (NYSE:SRE) Chairman and CEO, notes the transaction will streamline operations and fund growth in key U.S. markets, signaling a long-term partnership with KKR to advance LNG exports.

Upon closing, a KKR-led consortium will hold 65% of Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra retains 25% and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority takes the remaining 10%. The deal positions roughly 95% of Sempra's earnings to come from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This divestiture redirects Sempra's focus toward its regulated utilities, enabling increased investments in core operations and growth initiatives in states such as Texas and California. Analysts will monitor how the arrangement affects long-term growth and market position, while investors welcomed the announcement as shares rose nearly 5% on the news

SECTION 1 SEMPRA RECENT PRESS RELEASES

March 02: Sempra quarterly dividends up 1.9% sequentially from previous quarter

Sempra today announced a quarterly dividend of 65.75c per share for Q4/2025; up 1.9% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 64.50c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15.

March 02: Sempra quarterly dividends up 1.9% sequentially from previous quarter

Sempra today announced a quarterly dividend of 65.75c per share for Q4/2025; up 1.9% sequentially from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 64.50c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and the record date is Thursday, March 19, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, April 15.

November 10, 2025: Sempra quarterly dividends steady

Sempra today announced a quarterly dividend of 64.50c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q3/2025) dividend of 64.50c. The ex-dividend date is Thursday, December 11 and the record date is Thursday, December 11, 2025 and it is payable on Thursday, January 15, 2026.

SECTION 2 SEMPRA ACTIVITIES

Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Natural Resources Democrats Grill Interior Secretary Burgum

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-HH03-RXTB-R3X2-00000-00Natural Resources Democrats Grill Interior Secretary Burgum2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:01ZLexisNexisNatural Resources Democrats Grill Interior Secretary BurgumTargeted News ServiceWASHINGTON

Rep. Jared Huffman, D-California, ranking member of the House Natural Resources Committee, issued the following news release on May 13, 2026:

* * *

Natural Resources Democrats Grill Interior Secretary Burgum

Today, Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum testified in front of the House Natural Resources Committee in an attempt to defend Trump's abysmal track record and the rampant corruption raging throughout his department. Natural Resources Democrats grilled him about this administration's track record and their plans to permanently destroy our special places. Secretary Burgum dodged questions on everything from high gas prices and ceding renewable energy dominance to China to funneling taxpayer money into Trump's vanity projects.

Ranking Member Huffman delivered the following opening remarks:

"Since you were last here, things have unfortunately gotten worse for the department and a lot worse for the American people. President Trump is gutting funding and personnel in the agencies that manage our public lands, ordering what's left of the beleaguered national park service staff to whitewash history, shutting down projects that are ready to bring cheap, reliable, clean energy to millions of families who are struggling with skyrocketing utility bills and bullying the offshore wind industry into abandoning billions in investments that would bring even more affordable energy online.

"Mr. Secretary, at every turn, you've helped all this happen. You have treated the public lands and waters that you've been entrusted with like assets on a corporate balance sheet so that billionaires, foreign companies, and political donors can profit. You've enabled a culture of corruption where people in your agency are making decisions that line their own pockets and enrich their families and friends. Under your leadership, the Department of the Interior has been a billionaire's dream, but a nightmare for the public and a betrayal of our obligations to future generations. Families across the country right now can't even afford essentials like gas, health care, and groceries. But instead of trying to make their lives better, Trump has been obsessed with a raft of vanity projects, and you've been one of the principal enablers.

"Oil and gas prices are soaring. As America pulled back from global leadership on clean energy technology, China stepped right in to fill the void. And while your administration promotes oil, gas, and coal - the fuels of the 20th century - the rest of the world is looking to the future and rushing to buy EVs, batteries, solar panels, and wind turbines from China and others. Trump's policies have been terrible for our competition with China, terrible for working families trying to pay their bills.

"This is the most nakedly corrupt administration in American history. My oath of office requires me to be very candid about that.

"My colleagues and I have been working around the clock to keep up with this steady churn of scandal created by Trump and your Interior Department. But, Mr. Secretary, you and your agency have stonewalled us at every turn.

"What are you hiding? And for whose benefit? Mr. Secretary, we expect truthful answers to our questions. The American people deserve that."

Committee Democrats grilled the Secretary over the administration's efforts to sell out Americans, our environment, and our public lands while catering to billionaire polluters and corporations:

Cost of Living and Gas Prices

* RANKING MEMBER HUFFMAN: "Would you agree that most Americans are struggling right now to afford basic necessities of life because of rising costs?"

* SECRETARY BURGUM: "Uh, yeah."

* HUFFMAN: "I think most of us understand there's a struggle out there because of this administration's economic and energy policies. And while you are slashing just about every other part of the Department of the Interior's budget, including for national parks, you have spent a lot of the department's time, resources, and taxpayer dollars to serve Donald Trump's vanity. Mr. Secretary, while you're funneling billions into these vanity projects, gas is up by an average of $1.40 a gallon from last year. In March, President Trump posted this message. He said the United States is the largest oil producer in the world by far. So, "when oil prices go up, we make a lot of money." Who's the "we"?"

* BURGUM: "Well, it could be states, the federal government, the American people."

* HUFFMAN: "So, this is good for American families that they're paying $1.40 a gallon for gas."

* BURGUM: "I'm thrilled that they're paying, I'm thrilled that they're paying about a buck less than they were during the Biden administration. I mean, that's exciting."

* HUFFMAN: "Again, there's a real disconnect here. Americans are not celebrating this spike in gas prices that you seem to think is just great. The "we" that's making a lot more money are - let's be honest about it - it's the Big Oil billionaires."

Ceding Renewable Energy Dominance to China

* REP. DAVE MIN: "Do you agree that China is also trying to achieve total energy dominance?"

* BURGUM: "Uh, okay."

* MIN: "Do you know how much new energy China put online last year? They put on 434gw annually. 80% of the new energy that they put online last year was renewable. 80%. Meanwhile, the United States, under your, quote, total energy dominance strategy of rejecting renewable energy, put up 53gw of new energy last year, less than 10% of what China put up. Now, your clear bias against renewable energy in the face of all evidence is harming our national security, harming our ability to have energy independence, and putting us further and further behind the Chinese. And the cancellation of Total Energies' offshore wind leases last year is a case study on your department's economically illiterate and unlawful energy strategy."

Solar Permitting

* REP. SUSIE LEE: "Since for the past year, in my home state, there have been zero permits issued on solar. And with respect to following the law, the fact of the matter is a judge actually struck down the July 15th memo last month. So regardless of whether or not you agree with the court order, I guess my question is what's the immediate impact that that's having? Are you just not going to abide by the court order? Are you appealing it?"

* BURGUM: "Yeah, absolutely."

Affordable, Renewable Energy

* BURGUM: "Can you explain why the states that pursued the policies you're describing have the highest power prices in the country?"

* REP. SETH MAGAZINER: "Does North Dakota have one of the highest power prices in the country? You have one of the highest percentage of renewables. "

* BURGUM: "False!"

* MAGAZINER: "The data doesn't lie."

* BURGUM: "The data is lying."

White House Ballroom Spending:

* REP. MAXINE DEXTER: "Mr. Secretary, your budget cuts the national park service by $1 billion, a 32% cut. If you put this $1 billion towards our national parks instead of Trump's ballroom, you could avoid that cut that would benefit every single American. Mr. Secretary, at any time, did you object to this level of spending for the ballroom? Our budgets are values documents, Mr. Secretary. You said this in your opening comments that your priorities and your values are where you're spending money. And you are refusing to stand up to our president, who is investing in vanity projects instead of our federal lands and our national parks."

D.C. Reflecting Pool

* REP. JOE NEGUSE: "My understanding of the federal procurement rules is that a no-bid contract is reserved for situations where, quote, any delay would cause serious injury to the government. Your proposition is what? That there'd be serious injury to the government if this company didn't get the $13 million contract to do this particular project right now?"

* SECRETARY BURGUM: "Well, I think we do have a sense of urgency. I mean, we got handed a record amount of deferred maintenance. We had 19 fountains across the city that didn't work."

* NEGUSE: "That's the serious injury to the government?? Who picked this company? Because President Trump, a few months ago in a New York Times article, said, I have a guy who's unbelievable at doing swimming pools."

Park Maintenance

* REP. EMILY RANDALL: "You're proposing that the vast majority of funding for park construction be spent in D.C. at the direction of President Trump, not in our communities. This is wrong. It's misguided. Secretary Burgum, which do you think should be the higher priority: Fixing a bridge that is a safety hazard for hikers, or changing the bottom of the reflecting pool?"

* BURGUM: "Well, my answer would be both."

Great Lakes Economy

* REP. DEBBIE DINGELL: "You often talk about running government like a business. The Great Lakes fishery supports a $7 billion economy. The Great Lakes science center costs $15 million annually. I don't consider that a waste when it's protecting the drinking water of 30 million people and a $7 billion economy. The ROI is nearly 500 to 1. Do you believe it's good business to jeopardize the $7 billion economy and the region's source of clean drinking water, just to save that 15 million?"

Freedom 250

* HUFFMAN: "I want to just ask if you directed anyone at the National Park Service or anyone else at Interior to form the Freedom 250 LLC. Did that come from you?"

* BURGUM: "No. I'm not aware of the final decision maker on Freedom 250."

* HUFFMAN: "Would you get that information to us, please? I mean, I think that's significant."

* BURGUM: Sure.

Bureau of Ocean Energy Management--Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement Merger

* REP. LUZ RIVAS: "The merger of these two essential bureaus actually means less staff and less resources. Budgets will be slashed by 95 million and staff cut a staff or a staff cut of 224 employees. This means an inability for the country to enforce safety and environmental protections. You're also increasing offshore oil drilling. All of this being done just to ensure that Trump's polluting donors have another large check."

Park Passes

* REP. SARAH ELFRETH: "Interior now has a policy where you're invalidating park passes that have a sticker covering the president's face. This policy is now invalidating people's ability to access their public lands. I would just suggest that we rethink that policy for the purposes of ensuring people can access their public lands that they've helped pay for."

Tribal Nations and Sacred Lands

* REP. MELANIE STANSBURY: "And though you just said that you're prioritizing tribal self-government, your budget cuts self-governance compacts by $83 million, your budget makes over $1 billion in cuts to core tribal programs to BIA and BIE, it makes over 744 million cuts to BIA alone. It cuts tribal colleges by 150 million. It reduces school construction in tribal communities by 85%. And when you compare that with the other cuts in the president's budget and HUD and other programs, it eviscerates tribal housing programs, law enforcement, sanitation and water projects, irrigation projects, basic infrastructure, roads, economic development, and zeroes out basic education and cultural programs."

* REP. ADELITA GRIJALVA: "Because while as an administration you claim to respect tribal sovereignty and tribal heritage while sacred Indigenous sites are being destroyed under federal oversight, many are concerned with the Quitobaquito Spring as possibly being next on the destruction list for Trump's secondary border wall as it gets closer. So, will you commit that U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the National Park Service avoid Quitobaquito Springs and minimize impact by not using groundwater within five miles, and monitor those water levels closely as the border wall is being built."

* BURGUM: "Well, I.., that's a very specific thing that I won't jump ahead and commit to."

Some of Trump and Burgum's planned cuts:

* The National Park Service loses nearly a third of its budget. That is the agency running Yosemite, the Smokies, Acadia, Yellowstone, and 400 other sites. Less funding means fewer rangers, slower emergency response, closed bathrooms and visitor centers, trails that stop getting maintained, and longer waits to get in.

* The Fish and Wildlife Service is cut by about 20%. This is the agency behind the recovery of the bald eagle and the humpback whale, the protection of salmon runs and endangered species like the Florida manatee, and the national wildlife refuge system.

* The Bureau of Land Management loses about a third of its funding. BLM oversees 245 million acres of public lands, mostly across the West. These are the places where recreation actually happens: hunting, camping, climbing, off-roading, photography. Trump is cutting the funds needed to keep these places safe and open.

* The Bureau of Indian Affairs is cut by nearly 30%. BIA funds tribal schools, public safety in Indian Country, and the federal government's treaty obligations.

* Renewable energy funding is zeroed out. At the same time, the budget ramps up oil, gas, and mining development on public lands and offshore. Trump and Burgum are killing clean, affordable energy to prop up Big Oil billionaires.

Democrats have launched oversight investigations into:

* Trump using taxpayer money to build his gaudy vanity projects like the Arch de Trump and destroying of the East Wing to create his gilded ballroom while Americans struggle to afford daily life.

* DOI's efforts to whitewash and erase history and science at America's national parks.

* Trump's billion-dollar taxpayer-funded deal with a French oil company to kill two American offshore wind projects and clear the way for fossil fuels.

* Interior Department Associate Deputy Secretary Karen Budd-Falen for abusing her power to give her family a $3.5 million windfall.

* The admin's taxpayer-funded spending spree to buy up stakes in critical minerals companies to enrich themselves and their friends.

- NOTE: Rep. Dexter and Rep. Huffman moved to subpoena Don Trump Jr. over this, but Republicans blocked it.

* Water releases, dam takeovers, and transfers that will steal water from Northern California and divert it to SouthernCalifornia.
